zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Carpenter於大約1335年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾迪爾時，他的父親John Carpenter36歲，母親Mrs. John Carpenter23歲。他和Christina De Bailleul至少有3個兒子。他去世於1395年在英國英格蘭倫敦貝茲利亞斯聖 馬恩斯 邱奇享年61歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 馬丁 武特維其。

nameMeanings.name: Carpenter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): occupational name for a worker in wood, Norman French carpentier (from Late Latin carpentarius ‘cartwright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Zimmermann  , French   Charpentier  , Italian   Carpentieri  , or cognates and equivalents in various other languages.

variants.name: Zimmermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Hans, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Klaus, Gerd, Manfred, Wolfgang, Bernhard.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a carpenter, Middle High German zimberman (a compound of zimber, zim(m)er ‘timber, wood’ + man ‘man’), German Zimmermann.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Carpenter was born about 1335, in Dilwyn, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Carpenter, was 36 and his mother, Mrs. John Carpenter, was 23. He had at least 3 sons with Christina De Bailleul. He died in 1395, in St Martin's Church, Bexleyheath, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 61, and was buried in St Martin Outwich, London, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Carpenter(이)가 대략 1335년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 디w이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Carpenter(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. John Carpenter(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Christina De Bailleul(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1395년 영국 잉글랜드 런던 브스리히스 스트 마틴'스 c후르치에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 마틴 우트위치에 묻혔다.
